analysis_description: Round-3 tape snapshot at last timestamp (<=500000): Black-Scholes implied volatility (European call, S=VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid, K=strike from symbol, option mid in same price units) and vega per VEV; level-1 spread width. TTE mapping: historical CSV day column d in {0,1,2} corresponds to TTE = 8-d days per round3work/round3description.txt (offset consistent with the VEV_5000 worked example). Method: bisection IV; vega = S*sqrt(T)*phi(d1).
used_to_decide: ATM cluster VEV_5000/5100 show similar IV (~0.22-0.24) and combined vega ~230-330 vs wings; tightest quoted spreads at 5000/5100 vs deep OTM supports treating them as the liquid ATM bundle for a straddle-proxy. Informed v1: use per-leg IV for fair and add IV-vs-realized-vol shock alongside price-vs-theo mispricing.

analysis_description: ATM-cluster time-series analysis across all round-3 ticks (days 0-2): compute iv5000/iv5100, RV, shock=IVavg-RV, delta/vega, and spread gates. Includes threshold grid over mispricing-edge and shock-edge.
used_to_decide: Found per-leg-IV mispricing is nearly identity (~0), so v1 signals are driven by IV-vs-RV shock, not price-vs-theo edge. v3 (pure z-score) was falsified with large losses due to one-sided exposure. Chose v4 as safer continuation: retain v1 structure, tighten spread gate and shock threshold, reduce clip.

analysis_description: IV shock (ATM IVavg minus short-window extract RV) vs combined L1 spread on VEV_5000+VEV_5100, all ticks days 0-2. Buckets on spread sum: compare shock distribution in tight (sum<9) vs default ATM (~10) books. TTE per round3description: d->8-d. Method: same bisection IV and RV scaling as traders.
used_to_decide: Tightest books (spread sum<9) are a small subset (~720/30000 ticks) with slightly more negative median shock; median combined spread is 10, so v5’s gate=9 still captures most near-median liquidity. v5’s slightly lower SHOCK and higher clip raised total PnL vs v4 in backtest without abandoning the ATM bundle thesis.

analysis_description: Per-leg BS Greeks on ATM straddle proxy (VEV_5000 + VEV_5100 calls, IV from bisection on mids) at last tick ≤500k per day: straddle γ (sum of call gammas), straddle vega, straddle Θ (BS theta per year, r=0, reported as sum). TTE d->8-d per round3description.
used_to_decide: ATM cluster carries large combined vega (~230–330 by day) and material negative Θ, consistent with straddle/proxy PnL being IV- and time-sensitive. v6 (lower shock, looser misprice) and v7 (tighter spread 8.5) both lost vs v5, so v5 parameters remain the current best; next iterations should nudge in smaller steps or hold shock at 0.055.

analysis_description: ATM call IV smile skew within the 5000/5100 cluster: time series of IV(VEV_5100) - IV(VEV_5000) at each tick (BS bisection, same T as rounds). TTE d->8-d days.
used_to_decide: Skew is small in magnitude (median |ΔIV| a few bps) on day 0, turns negative by day 2, suggesting 5100-IV is not systematically richer than 5000-IV; coordinated equal clip on both remains reasonable. v8 (clip 12) and v9 (spread 9.2) did not beat v5; clip increases alone do not add enough edge in this backtest; best remains v5 (gate 9, clip 11, shock 0.055).

analysis_description: ATM leg-tilt signal from skew iv5100-iv5000 over all ticks (days 0-2): sign ratio and mean per day to decide whether equal vs tilted sizing is justified.
used_to_decide: Skew sign flips by day (day0 mostly positive, day1/day2 mostly negative), so static tilt is unstable across days. v10 (tilted from best parent) and v11 (tilted from random older parent) both underperform equal-leg v5, so equal coordinated sizing remains preferred.

analysis_description: Family10 pivot: quadratic smile in log-moneyness with spread-weighted LS; compare plain WLS vs robust winsorized IRLS-WLS on residual tails and PnL under --match-trades worse.
used_to_decide: Robust fit slightly improved p90/p95 residual tails vs plain but worsened extreme tail p99/max in this setup; both robust (v12) and plain control (v13) produced strongly negative PnL, so this family/threshold combo is falsified versus existing v5 baseline.

analysis_description: Required duplicate-check + adjusted variant test. Parent index-10 branch already explored quadratic-logm WLS with broad gate grids; my adjusted variant applies strict outlier-suppression gates (high residual/shock thresholds, low clip) while comparing robust IRLS-winsorized WLS vs plain WLS under identical strict execution.
used_to_decide: Because parent already explored broad smile/WLS execution/gate sweeps, duplicate risk on the original request was high. I switched to strict-gated robust/plain controls. On this branch robust (v14) slightly beats plain (v15), but both remain well below class target and only modestly above parent family outcomes (~53k).

analysis_description: Underlying propagation analysis by strike from Round3 tapes: contemporaneous beta dC/dS, up/down asymmetry, and post-shock spread response; then family10 robust/plain variants add an explained-move suppression filter using expected bundle move from beta_5000+beta_5100.
used_to_decide: Core strikes have sizable dC/dS (~0.41 avg over 5000-5400), minimal asymmetry, and little spread widening after top-decile extract shocks. Implemented filter to avoid trading when ATM bundle move is mostly explained by concurrent extract move. v16/v17 improved strongly over v12/v13 but remain below prior strict-gated v14/v15 and far below class bar.

analysis_description: Lead/lag: Pearson corr dC_t vs dS_{t-k} and dS_t vs dC_{t-k} for k=0,1,2,3,5 on Round3; simultaneous k=0 ~0.75+ for 5000/5100, higher lags near zero. Vega at median: ~118 for 5000. Used to set vega-scaled |mid-fair| floors and |dS|-scaled shock edge instead of lag trading.
used_to_decide: v18 parent=best local family10 v14. Gates now require price mispricing to exceed vega-appropriate minimum and raise IV-shock bar when |dS| is large, reducing spurious short during pure co-move. Backtest 55009 vs v14 53078 total.

analysis_description: Round-3: robust smile (same fit as v18) on each tick, compute |sum(mid5000+mid5100)-smile_fair| vs combined L1 spread. Buckets: tight (ss<=8) show higher median mispricing; wide (ss>11) lower. Corr of mis/spread with ss is negative. Used to set spread_adj on vega floor and small shock bar widening on wide books.
used_to_decide: Tight 5000+5100 books carry larger structural smile residuals per tick, so v19 raises the effective edge floor in those regimes. v19 total 55221 > v18 55009 under worse-match; still far from 800k bar (would need multi-VEV execution and/or parent full smile stack).

analysis_description: Offline subsample (day 0, every 10th tick, robust smile like v19): Pearson corr between |bundle mispricing| and BS straddle gamma (g5000+g5100) is ~0.25; see analysis_outputs/mispricing_vs_gamma_straddle_v20.json. TTE from round3description via hist day d -> 8-d.
used_to_decide: v20 adds a mis_floor term proportional to straddle gamma (GAMMA_MISPRICE_PER_GAMMA * (g0+g1)) on top of v19. Backtest PnL and fills matched v19 because entries are still dominated by the IV-shock OR branch (shock not gated by mis_floor), so the gamma term only would affect pure misprice-driven trades.

analysis_description: Round-3 propagation deep-dive for shock entries under robust smile family10. Two analyses: (1) shock-vs-mis sign conflict against explained bundle move residual |d(bundle)-0.82*dS|; (2) next-tick proxy quality for shock-triggered side split by |dS| and combined ATM spread (5000+5100). TTE mapping kept d->8-d from round3description.
used_to_decide: Shock and mis signs conflict on ~99% of shock ticks, explaining why mis_floor tweaks had little effect. Next-tick proxy improved with larger |dS| and degraded sharply when combined spread was wide (>~11). v21 therefore keeps robust smile + gamma floor but changes shock execution logic: require minimum |dS| for shock-only entries and raise shock threshold further in wide-book regimes.

analysis_description: Read round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt and outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt: joint gate s5200<=2 and s5300<=2 (L1 ask-bid) aligns with higher mean K-step forward extract mid change and lower t-stat outside that box. Repro script: analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3.py. Merged origin/main brings round 3 day 3 tape; backtests now run days 0-3 unless constrained by CLI.
used_to_decide: v22 wires the joint 5200/5300 tight spread as a risk-on sizing signal (clip 4->5 and larger extract hedge cap when both <=2), not a hard trade gate (hard gate + day_3 data test was strongly negative). Hydrogel disabled to focus PnL on VEV+extract. Worse and all match modes recorded; outputs were identical for this run.

analysis_description: Authoritative read: round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt + ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES.txt (Sonic: both 5200/5300 spread at or below 2; inclineGod: spread/spread not just price). Folder outputs: r3_tight_spread_summary.txt (K=20 forward mid higher when joint tight). Implementation: no legacy family10 logic—joint gate on extract spread cap + EMA fade on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT in tight regime; voucher legs not used for orders in v23 to respect execution caveat.
used_to_decide: Ship a clean thesis pivot: trade extract only when the Sonic joint gate and tradeable extract spread hold; EMA reversion. backtest_worse 4912 round3 days0-3; all 5555. Far below 200k/day floor—next: add on-voucher execution that uses gate without naive momentum, or calibrate to folder K-step stats under worse fills.

analysis_description: Test linking folder K=20 forward-mid narrative to a live rule: S(t)-S(t-20) momentum when joint 5200/5300 tight, with equal 5200+5300+extract direction.
used_to_decide: Falsified under worse and all: large negative (worse -28430). Do not use raw signed momentum as a standalone directional rule with this execution; mid-forward result does not transfer. Prefer mean-reversion or a different VEV quoting model in later iterations.

analysis_description: v23 extract MR extended per STRATEGY.txt optional layer (short-hold long/short both strikes with extract) using same dev signal: equal 5200+5300 with extract when joint tight; optional clip boost when s52+s53<=2 (spread--spread / tightness of the joint). Data remains round3work/vouchers_final_strategy definitions.
used_to_decide: v25 worse 14716 vs v23 4912: adding balanced voucher legs in tight regime helps total but VEV_5200 PnL negative on some days in backtest, while 5300 positive. Next: per-leg or single-leg v26.

analysis_description: Per-voucher execution test: keep joint gate (both s<=2) but only trade VEV_5300 in direction of the same EMA reversion on extract; VEV_5200 spread still observed to define regime, no 5200 orders. Aligns with inclineGod 'each contract' spread/price but uses empirical fill asymmetry from v25.
used_to_decide: v26 worse 18034, all 18678, best in pivot line so far; still <<200k/day. Continue with scaling/tighter s_ex, or add second venue logic on 5200 with lighter size for hedge.
